plugin.name: IKE Maven Plugin
plugin.description: IDE-discoverable Maven goals wrapping ike-build-tools bash scripts. The plugin provides discoverability; the scripts hold the logic.

mojo.description:
Generate documentation from AsciiDoc sources using AsciidoctorJ. Replaces asciidoctor-maven-plugin with a single goal that handles multiple backends in one execution. Extensions (Koncept inline macro, glossary postprocessor) are registered programmatically with backend-aware safety — the Prawn PDF backend does not receive postprocessor extensions that would crash JRuby.
Output validation is built in: unresolved attributes, missing includes, broken cross-references, and missing images are detected and optionally fail the build.
Usage: <execution> <id>generate-docs</id> <goals><goal>asciidoc</goal></goals> <configuration> <skipHtml>false</skipHtml> <skipPrawn>false</skipPrawn> <validate>true</validate> </configuration> </execution>

parameter.name: attributes
parameter.description: Additional AsciiDoc attributes merged with defaults.

parameter.name: baseDir
parameter.description: Base directory for relative includes.

parameter.name: diagramServerUrl
parameter.description: Kroki diagram server URL.

parameter.name: documentName
parameter.description: Output document base name.

parameter.name: htmlToc
parameter.description: TOC placement for HTML output.

parameter.name: htmlTocLevels
parameter.description: TOC depth for HTML output.

parameter.name: outputDirectory
parameter.description: Root output directory. Backends write to subdirectories.

parameter.name: pdfFontsDir
parameter.description: Font directory for Prawn PDF.

parameter.name: pdfTheme
parameter.description: Prawn theme name.

parameter.name: pdfThemeDir
parameter.description: Prawn theme directory.

parameter.name: requires
parameter.description: Ruby libraries to require. Defaults to asciidoctor-diagram.

parameter.name: sharedAsciidocDir
parameter.description: Shared docinfo directory.

parameter.name: skip
parameter.description: Skip all execution.

parameter.name: skipDocbook
parameter.description: Skip DocBook 5 backend.

parameter.name: skipHtml
parameter.description: Skip HTML5 backend.

parameter.name: skipHtmlSingle
parameter.description: Skip single-file HTML generation.

parameter.name: skipPrawn
parameter.description: Skip Prawn PDF backend.

parameter.name: sourceDirectory
parameter.description: AsciiDoc source directory.

parameter.name: sourceDocumentName
parameter.description: Source document name (e.g., index.adoc). When set, only this file is converted. When null, all .adoc files in the source directory are converted.

parameter.name: strict
parameter.description: Fail the build on any validation error.

parameter.name: validate
parameter.description: Validate output for unresolved attributes, broken xrefs, etc.

mojo.description: Compute the cascade (propagation set) from a changed component. Shows all components that transitively depend on the given component and would need rebuilding after a change. mvn ike:cascade -Dcomponent=tinkar-core

parameter.name: component
parameter.description: The component that changed. Prompted if omitted.

parameter.name: manifest
parameter.description: Path to workspace.yaml. If not set, searches upward from the current directory. Package-private for test access.

mojo.description:
Defensive git hook — warns when a branch is created or switched outside the workspace tooling. Intended to be called from a post-checkout git hook: #!/bin/sh mvn -q ike:check-branch -- "$@" In workspace mode, compares the current branch to the expected branch in workspace.yaml and warns on mismatch. Provides copy-pasteable undo commands.
In bare mode (no workspace.yaml), silently exits — nothing to check.
Never blocks — warnings only. Always exits 0.

parameter.name: manifest
parameter.description: Path to workspace.yaml. If not set, searches upward from the current directory. Package-private for test access.

mojo.description:
Create an immutable checkpoint. A checkpoint is a lightweight, tagged snapshot — no release branch ceremony, no GPG signing. The workflow:
* Derive checkpoint version from current SNAPSHOT * Set POM versions, resolve ${project.version} * Build and verify * Commit, tag with checkpoint/<version> * Deploy to Nexus (no GPG) * Optionally deploy site to immutable checkpoint URL * Restore SNAPSHOT version Usage: mvn ike:checkpoint mvn ike:checkpoint -DdryRun=true mvn ike:checkpoint -DdeploySite=false

Remove a deployed site directory from the server. Useful for manual cleanup of stale snapshot or checkpoint sites that were not automatically removed by feature-finish or release.
Usage: mvn ike:clean-site -DsiteType=snapshot -Dbranch=feature/old-work mvn ike:clean-site -DsiteType=checkpoint -DsiteVersion=7-checkpoint.20260101.1 mvn ike:clean-site -DsiteType=snapshot # cleans current branch's snapshot

parameter.name: branch
parameter.description: Branch whose snapshot site should be cleaned. Defaults to the current git branch. Only used when siteType=snapshot.

parameter.name: dryRun
parameter.description: Show plan without executing.

parameter.name: siteType
parameter.description: Site type to clean: snapshot, checkpoint, or release.

parameter.name: siteVersion
parameter.description: Version of the checkpoint site to clean. Only used when siteType=checkpoint.

mojo.description:
Copy rendered HTML docs and assets into the Maven site output directory. Copies HTML files plus supporting assets (SVG diagrams, PNG/JPG images, CSS stylesheets) from the Asciidoctor generated-docs directory into target/site/docs/ so they are deployed alongside the Maven site.
Replaces: copy-docs-to-site.sh
Usage: mvn ike:copy-docs -DgeneratedDocsDir=target/generated-docs/html -DsiteDir=target/site

parameter.name: generatedDocsDir
parameter.description: Directory containing rendered HTML and assets.

parameter.name: siteDir
parameter.description: Maven site output directory (e.g., target/site).

mojo.description: Composite workspace dashboard — one invocation, full overview. Runs verify + status + cascade-from-dirty in a single pass, loading the manifest once and iterating components once. This is the recommended "morning standup" command. mvn ike:dashboard

parameter.name: manifest
parameter.description: Path to workspace.yaml. If not set, searches upward from the current directory. Package-private for test access.

mojo.description:
Generate and deploy the Maven site to a versioned URL. This goal deploys the project site to one of three location types under ike.komet.sh:
* release — overwritten on each release * snapshot — versioned by git branch (e.g., snapshot/main/, snapshot/feature/my-work/) * checkpoint — immutable, versioned subdirectory Every deployment uses a stage-and-swap strategy: SCP uploads to a .staging directory, then an atomic rename replaces the live directory. This eliminates stale files from previous deploys (SCP alone only copies, never deletes) and avoids a window where the site is missing.
Usage: mvn ike:deploy-site -DsiteType=snapshot mvn ike:deploy-site -DsiteType=snapshot -Dbranch=feature/kec-march-25 mvn ike:deploy-site -DsiteType=checkpoint -DsiteVersion=7-checkpoint.20260228.1 mvn ike:deploy-site -DsiteType=release

parameter.name: branch
parameter.description: Git branch for snapshot deploys. Defaults to the current branch. Used to derive the snapshot subdirectory (e.g., main → snapshot/main/).

parameter.name: dryRun
parameter.description: Show plan without executing.

parameter.name: siteVersion
parameter.description: Explicit site version for checkpoint deploys. Defaults to the POM version.

parameter.name: skipBuild
parameter.description: Skip the mvn clean verify step.

parameter.name: skipSwap
parameter.description: Skip the atomic swap (deploy directly over the live directory). Not recommended — leaves stale files from previous deploys and causes a brief window where the site is incomplete.

mojo.description:
Finish a feature branch — merge back to main across components. Workspace mode (workspace.yaml found):
For each component in the specified group that is currently on the feature branch:
* Validates the working tree is clean * If the version is branch-qualified, strips the qualifier (e.g., 1.2.0-my-feature-SNAPSHOT → 1.2.0-SNAPSHOT) * Commits the version change * Checks out the target branch (default: main) * Merges the feature branch with --no-ff * Tags the merge point as merge/feature/<name> * Optionally pushes to origin * Updates workspace.yaml branch fields back to targetBranch Bare mode (no workspace.yaml):
Merges the feature branch in the current repo only.
Components are processed in reverse topological order so that leaf components (komet-desktop) merge first, and foundation components (ike-parent) merge last. mvn ike:feature-finish -Dfeature=shield-terminology -Dgroup=core mvn ike:feature-finish -Dfeature=kec-march-25 -Dgroup=studio -Dpush=true mvn ike:feature-finish -Dfeature=kec-march-25 -DdryRun=true

parameter.name: dryRun
parameter.description: Show plan without executing.

parameter.name: feature
parameter.description: Feature name. Expects branch feature/<name>. Prompted if omitted.

parameter.name: group
parameter.description: Restrict to a named group or component. Default: all cloned.

parameter.name: manifest
parameter.description: Path to workspace.yaml. If not set, searches upward from the current directory. Package-private for test access.

parameter.name: push
parameter.description: Push to origin after merge. Default: false (safety).

parameter.name: targetBranch
parameter.description: Target branch to merge into. Default: main.

mojo.description:
Start a coordinated feature branch across workspace components. Creates a feature branch with a consistent name across the specified components (or group), optionally setting branch-qualified SNAPSHOT versions in each POM.
Workspace mode (workspace.yaml found):
* Validates the working tree is clean * Creates branch feature/<name> from the current HEAD * If the component has a Maven version, sets a branch-qualified version (e.g., 1.2.0-my-feature-SNAPSHOT) * Commits the version change * Updates workspace.yaml branch fields for all branched components * Commits the workspace.yaml change Bare mode (no workspace.yaml):
* Creates the feature branch in the current repo only * Sets version-qualified SNAPSHOT in the current repo's POMs Components are processed in topological order so that upstream dependencies get their new versions first. mvn ike:feature-start -Dfeature=shield-terminology -Dgroup=core mvn ike:feature-start -Dfeature=kec-march-25 -Dgroup=studio mvn ike:feature-start -Dfeature=doc-refresh -Dgroup=docs -DskipVersion=true

parameter.name: dryRun
parameter.description: Show plan without executing.

parameter.name: feature
parameter.description: Feature name. Branch will be feature/<name>. Prompted if omitted.

parameter.name: group
parameter.description: Restrict to a named group or component. Default: all cloned.

parameter.name: manifest
parameter.description: Path to workspace.yaml. If not set, searches upward from the current directory. Package-private for test access.

parameter.name: skipVersion
parameter.description: Skip POM version qualification. Useful for document projects that don't have versioned artifacts.

mojo.description:
Remove bare <rect/> elements from generated HTML files. Mermaid generates <rect/> elements (no attributes) as non-functional placeholders inside flowchart label groups. These elements violate the SVG spec (missing required width/height) and cause warnings in Prince and other PDF renderers.
Replaces: fix-inline-svg.sh
Usage: mvn ike:fix-svg -DhtmlFile=target/generated-docs/html/document.html

parameter.name: htmlFile
parameter.description: The HTML file to process.

parameter.name: skip
parameter.description: Skip execution.

mojo.description:
Generate a Bill of Materials POM from another module's dependency management. Reads the <dependencyManagement> entries from a source module (default: ike-parent) in the reactor and writes a standalone BOM POM with all versions resolved to literals. The generated POM replaces the stub POM for install/deploy, so external consumers get a fully populated BOM without any manual maintenance.
Bind this goal to a POM-packaged stub module in the reactor, ordered after the source module and the plugin module: <plugin> <groupId>network.ike</groupId> <artifactId>ike-maven-plugin</artifactId> <executions> <execution> <id>generate-bom</id> <goals><goal>generate-bom</goal></goals> </execution> </executions> </plugin>

parameter.name: project
parameter.description: The current project (injected by Maven).

parameter.name: reactorProjects
parameter.description: Reactor projects (injected by Maven).

parameter.name: sourceArtifactId
parameter.description: Artifact ID of the reactor module whose <dependencyManagement> entries should be copied into the generated BOM.

mojo.description: Print the workspace dependency graph. Displays all components in topological order with their dependencies. Optionally outputs DOT format for Graphviz rendering. mvn ike:graph mvn ike:graph -Dformat=dot

parameter.name: format
parameter.description: Output format: "text" (default) or "dot" (Graphviz DOT).

parameter.name: manifest
parameter.description: Path to workspace.yaml. If not set, searches upward from the current directory. Package-private for test access.

mojo.description: Displays available IKE build tool goals. See also: IKE Pipeline <https://github.com/IKE-Network/ike-pipeline>

Clone and initialize workspace components from the manifest. Three initialization modes per component:
* Already cloned — directory has .git/; skip. * Syncthing working tree — directory exists but no .git/. Initializes git in-place: git init, adds the remote, fetches, and resets to match the remote branch. This preserves file content synced from another machine. * Fresh clone — no directory; runs git clone. Components are initialized in topological (dependency) order. mvn ike:init mvn ike:init -Dgroup=studio mvn ike:init -Dgroup=docs

parameter.name: group
parameter.description: Restrict to a named group (or single component). Default: all.

parameter.name: manifest
parameter.description: Path to workspace.yaml. If not set, searches upward from the current directory. Package-private for test access.

mojo.description:
Inject navigation breadcrumbs and theme overrides into JaCoCo HTML reports. Finds all HTML files in the target directory and:
* Prepends a "back to project site" link in the breadcrumb div * Injects a CSS link to ike-theme.css for visual alignment with the project's Maven site skin * Writes ike-theme.css into the JaCoCo resources directory Usage: mvn ike:inject-breadcrumb -DtargetDir=target/site/jacoco

parameter.name: label
parameter.description: Display label for the breadcrumb link.

parameter.name: link
parameter.description: Relative URL for the breadcrumb link.

parameter.name: targetDir
parameter.description: Directory containing JaCoCo HTML reports.

mojo.description:
Patch stock DocBook XSL stylesheets to suppress Saxon warnings. Applies two targeted fixes to the downloaded DocBook XSL 1.79.2 distribution:
* fo/docbook.xsl — removes the direct include of ../common/utility.xsl which creates a diamond import (Saxon SXWN9019: included or imported more than once). * fo/math.xsl — removes the dead <xsl:variable name="output.delims"> block that is computed but never used (Saxon SXWN9001). Replaces: patch-docbook-xsl.sh
Usage: mvn ike:patch-docbook -DdocbookDir=target/docbook-xsl

parameter.name: docbookDir
parameter.description: Root directory of the unpacked DocBook XSL distribution.

mojo.description: Pull latest changes across workspace components. Runs git pull --rebase in each cloned component directory in topological order (dependencies first). Uninitialized components are skipped with a warning. mvn ike:pull mvn ike:pull -Dgroup=studio

parameter.name: group
parameter.description: Restrict to a named group (or single component). Default: all.

parameter.name: manifest
parameter.description: Path to workspace.yaml. If not set, searches upward from the current directory. Package-private for test access.

mojo.description:
Full release: build, deploy, tag, merge, and bump to next SNAPSHOT. This goal automates the complete release workflow in one command. All local git work completes before any external action, so a deploy failure leaves the local repository in a consistent state and the deploy can be retried manually.
Local phase (idempotent):
* Validate prerequisites (branch, clean worktree) * Create release/<version> branch * Set POM version to release version * Build and verify * Build site (pre-flight — catches javadoc errors early) * Commit, tag * Restore ${project.version}, merge to main * Bump to next SNAPSHOT version, verify, commit External phase (most reversible first, irreversible last):
* Deploy site from tagged commit (overwritable — safe to retry) * Deploy to Nexus from tagged commit (irreversible — last) * Push tag and main to origin * Create GitHub Release Usage: mvn ike:release (auto-derives version from POM), or override with mvn ike:release -DreleaseVersion=2
See also: CheckpointMojo

Scan renderer log files for error patterns and print a summary. Finds all renderer-*.log files in the configured directory, counts lines matching common error patterns (error, fatal, exception, failed, not found), and prints a one-line summary per renderer.
Never fails the build — renderer exit codes already fail the build if appropriate. This goal is informational only.
Replaces: scan-renderer-logs.sh
Usage: mvn ike:scan-logs -DlogsDir=target/generated-docs

parameter.name: logsDir
parameter.description: Directory containing renderer-*.log files.

mojo.description: Show git status across all workspace components. For each component directory that exists, reports the current branch, short SHA, and whether the working tree is clean or dirty. Missing directories are flagged as "not cloned". mvn ike:status mvn ike:status -Dgroup=studio

parameter.name: group
parameter.description: Restrict to a named group (or single component). Default: all.

parameter.name: manifest
parameter.description: Path to workspace.yaml. If not set, searches upward from the current directory. Package-private for test access.

mojo.description:
Generate Syncthing .stignore files for the workspace. Syncthing should sync source files across a developer's own machines but must NOT sync build artifacts or git metadata (those are machine-specific). This goal generates two files:
* Workspace-level .stignore — ignores target/, .git/, IDE files, and OS metadata within each component directory. * Per-component .stignore — same patterns, placed in each cloned component for standalone Syncthing folders. The generated file is deterministic (sorted, no timestamps) so running the goal twice produces identical output — no Syncthing churn. mvn ike:stignore

parameter.name: manifest
parameter.description: Path to workspace.yaml. If not set, searches upward from the current directory. Package-private for test access.

mojo.description: Verify workspace manifest consistency. Checks that all dependency references resolve, no cycles exist, all group members are valid, and all component types are defined. Fails the build if any errors are found. mvn ike:verify

parameter.name: manifest
parameter.description: Path to workspace.yaml. If not set, searches upward from the current directory. Package-private for test access.

mojo.description:
Record a workspace checkpoint — a consistent snapshot of all component SHAs, branches, and versions. Writes a YAML checkpoint file to checkpoints/ in the workspace root and optionally tags each component's current commit.
This is the workspace-level checkpoint (multi-repo). For single-repo checkpoints, use ike:checkpoint instead. mvn ike:ws-checkpoint -Dname=sprint-42 mvn ike:ws-checkpoint -Dname=pre-release -Dtag=true

parameter.name: manifest
parameter.description: Path to workspace.yaml. If not set, searches upward from the current directory. Package-private for test access.

parameter.name: name
parameter.description: Checkpoint name. Used in filename and tags. Prompted if omitted.

parameter.name: push
parameter.description: Push tags to origin. Only applies when tag=true.

parameter.name: tag
parameter.description: Tag each component with checkpoint/<name>/<component>.

mojo.description:
Workspace-level release — release all dirty checked-out components in topological order. Scans checked-out components for commits since their last release tag. Dirty components are topologically sorted and released in dependency order. After each release, downstream parent version references are updated automatically.
What it does, per component:
* Detect latest release tag (v*) * Check for commits since that tag * If dirty: run mvn ike:release in that component's directory * After release: update parent version in downstream components The cascade is self-limiting: only checked-out components with changes since their last release are candidates. Components not present in the aggregator are not considered. mvn ike:ws-release # release all dirty components mvn ike:ws-release -DdryRun=true # show what would be released mvn ike:ws-release -Dgroup=core # only components in the core group mvn ike:ws-release -Dcomponent=ike-pipeline # release one specific component

parameter.name: component
parameter.description: Release only this specific component.

parameter.name: dryRun
parameter.description: Preview what would be released without executing.

parameter.name: group
parameter.description: Release only components in this group.

parameter.name: manifest
parameter.description: Path to workspace.yaml. If not set, searches upward from the current directory. Package-private for test access.

parameter.name: push
parameter.description: Push releases to remote. Passed through to ike:release.

parameter.name: skipCheckpoint
parameter.description: Skip the pre-release checkpoint.

mojo.description:
Synchronize workspace.yaml branch fields with actual git branches. Two modes:
* Default (from=repos): read actual branches from each cloned component and update workspace.yaml to match reality. * from=manifest: read workspace.yaml branch fields and switch each cloned component to the declared branch. mvn ike:ws-sync # update yaml from repos mvn ike:ws-sync -Dfrom=manifest # switch repos to match yaml

parameter.name: dryRun
parameter.description: Show plan without executing.

parameter.name: from
parameter.description: Sync direction: repos (default) updates workspace.yaml from actual branches; manifest switches repos to match workspace.yaml.

parameter.name: manifest
parameter.description: Path to workspace.yaml. If not set, searches upward from the current directory. Package-private for test access.
